pages.raw_text:
1.-THE Northern Pacific has prom
Used me that the Howard street
crossing will be fixed up immediate
ly," said Commissioner ONO this
morning. New signal blocks will
be put in and kept in order"


After playing football with
street car on the Howard street
crossing, the Northern Pacific
seems inclined to consider the
city's mild request of months ago
that ordinary precautions be taken
to avoid accidents at the crossing.


No compulsion has been used, of
course. The railroad Is willing to
make some sort of repara ton for
taking such liberties with a w. w.
P. street car. All of which lifts q
vast burden from the commission
er's backs for an unprotected rail-
road crossing in the heart of the
city is matter of some concern to
folks in Office. But If the COM
pany won't pay any attention to po-
lite requests regarding the matter,
what can the poor commissioners
do?


Merely fold their arms and wait,
apparently, until something hap
pens, meanwhile hoping that it
won't be very serious. Fortunately,
yesterdays collision falls in the
latter category, through no care of
the Northern Pacific nor the city.


This seems to be the sort Of
policy to be expected in Spokane
